dimensions.name: Requirements Met
dimensions.gaps_to_10: Pure logic layer (damage formula, status effects, catch formula, exp/level-up, AI) is solid and all 68 tests pass. However the UX-facing requirements are substantially missing: (1) ITEM action non-functional in UI, (2) battle events never surfaced in dialog box, (3) attack/entrance/faint animations absent, (4) Pokeball throw/wobble animations absent, (5) EXP bar animation missing, (6) level-up message never shown. The Requirements section explicitly lists all of these.

dimensions.name: Error Handling
dimensions.gaps_to_10: BattleRenderer handles null state gracefully. EncounterSystem has try/catch. GameEngine has red-screen error recovery. BattleSystem.processTurn guards against null/battleOver. Minor gap: aiMove variable in BattleSystemCore.ts ITEM branch is declared but never used (the AI still gets a turn but the variable is computed and discarded).

dimensions.name: UX Completeness
dimensions.gaps_to_10: Core battle UX is broken: (1) battle.events is populated by processTurn but never consumed — currentMessage is never updated, so type-effectiveness text, move names, status messages, and level-up announcements are all silently swallowed. (2) ITEM and POKEMON choices in the action menu have no handler — selecting ITEM does nothing, making Pokeball throwing impossible through the game UI. (3) All BattleRenderer.ts animations are stubs: attack sprite nudge, white-flash 3x, enemy slide-in entrance, faint drop+fade, parabolic Pokeball throw, and wobble are all missing. (4) EXP bar uses raw pokemon.experience in PanelRenderer (not anim.playerDisplayExp), so there is no fill animation.

dimensions.name: Code Quality
dimensions.gaps_to_10: Clean modular split (DamageCalc, StatusEffects, CatchSystem, ExpSystem, MoveSelection, PanelRenderer, MenuRenderer). Files stay under 150 lines. Types are well-defined. Minor issues: battle phase state is mutated inside render() (BattleRenderer.renderIntro sets battle.battlePhase = 'SELECT_ACTION'), which is a side-effect anti-pattern. The ANIMATING/ENEMY_TURN/CHECK_END phases are defined in BattleTypes.ts but never set in the real game loop.

dimensions.name: Test Coverage
dimensions.gaps_to_10: All 68 tests pass. Required test files (damage-formula, battle-system, catch-formula) are present and cover the specified scenarios including edge cases (paralysis boundary, PP=0 Struggle, wobble count 0, ultra ball multiplier). Gap: the 'IMMUNE BattleEvent emitted' test only calls isImmune() directly rather than calling processTurn() and asserting a BattleEvent with type='IMMUNE' is returned — a narrow miss from the spec wording.

dimensions.name: Security
dimensions.gaps_to_10: No hardcoded API keys. No user input flows to DB or LLM. Canvas rendering is safe. Minor: no input sanitization on pokemon nicknames (not a concern for a local game).

issues.description: ITEM and POKEMON choices in the action menu have no handler. handleActionMenu() only handles 'FIGHT' and 'RUN'; selecting ITEM or POKEMON does nothing and the cursor returns to SELECT_ACTION. The player can never throw a Pokeball through normal gameplay, breaking the 'Selecting ITEM → POKÉBALL → throw animation → caught!' requirement.
issues.fix_guidance: Add an 'else if (choice === 'ITEM')' branch that creates a PlayerAction { type: 'ITEM', itemId: 1 } (Pokeball as default), calls processTurn(), pushes events, then sets battlePhase to 'ANIMATING' or processes the catch result. The BattleSystem needs an item-selection sub-menu (at minimum a hard-coded Pokeball for MVP) so the player can actually attempt a catch.

issues.description: Battle events returned by processTurn() are pushed to battle.events but that array is never consumed. currentMessage is never updated from events, so the player never sees: move names used, 'It's super effective!', status condition inflicted, damage amounts, 'You gained X EXP!', or 'BULBASAUR grew to Lv.6!' messages. The dialog box is permanently frozen at the initial message.
issues.fix_guidance: After calling processTurn(), iterate the returned events array and translate them into sequential currentMessage strings that the player sees one at a time (with awaitingInput=true between messages). At minimum: MOVE_USED → '{Name} used {Move}!', DAMAGE with effectiveness>1 → 'It's super effective!', DAMAGE with effectiveness<1 → 'Not very effective...', IMMUNE → 'It had no effect!', LEVEL_UP → '{Name} grew to Lv.{N}!', EXP_GAIN → '{Name} gained {N} EXP!', FAINT → '{Name} fainted!'. Store pendingMessages: string[] and dequeue one per input press.

issues.description: Attack animation is not implemented. The task requires: attacker sprite nudges 8px toward opponent over 100ms then returns; defender sprite flashes white 3× (3×80ms on/off). Currently sprites are static colored rectangles that never move or flash.
issues.fix_guidance: Add an animation state object in BattleRenderer: { phase: 'IDLE'|'ATTACKER_LUNGE'|'DEFENDER_FLASH'|..., startTime, flashCount }. When a MOVE_USED event is queued, transition to ATTACKER_LUNGE; interpolate sprite offset over 100ms then return; then begin DEFENDER_FLASH cycling fill color between white and normal 3 times over 240ms. Use Date.now() timestamps already present in the renderer.

issues.description: Enemy entrance animation is not implemented. The task requires the enemy sprite to slide in from the right edge (x=160) to its target position over 300ms when the battle begins. Currently the sprite is drawn at a fixed position from frame 1.
issues.fix_guidance: During INTRO phase, track an enemySpriteX offset starting at 160 and lerping to the target position (88) over 300ms using Date.now(). Apply this offset when drawing the enemy sprite placeholder rectangle. This also sets the visual expectation that the sprite 'arrived'.

issues.description: Faint animation is not implemented. The task requires the fainted Pokemon sprite to drop 16px downward and fade alpha 1→0 over 400ms when a FAINT event occurs.
issues.fix_guidance: Add a faint animation state tracking which side is fainting and the start time. When a FAINT BattleEvent is encountered in the pending-event queue, enter FAINT_ANIM phase; compute progress=(now-start)/400; draw sprite at baseY+16*progress with globalAlpha=1-progress; after 400ms proceed to next event.

issues.description: Pokeball throw animation (parabolic arc, 500ms) and wobble animation (±15° per shake, 4 shakes) are not implemented. This makes Pokeball catching entirely non-visual even after fixing the ITEM UI handler.
issues.fix_guidance: Add POKEBALL_THROW and POKEBALL_WOBBLE animation phases. For the throw: compute a quadratic bezier from player-panel center to enemy-panel center over 500ms, drawing a small circle at the interpolated position. For wobble: per shake, animate canvas rotation ±15° (sin wave over ~200ms per shake). Then show CATCH_RESULT message. See BattleTypes.ts CatchAttemptEvent.shakeCount for the number of wobbles.

issues.description: EXP bar animation is not implemented. The task requires animateFill(target, 600ms). PanelRenderer.drawPlayerPanel() draws the EXP bar using pokemon.experience directly, bypassing anim.playerDisplayExp entirely. The bar jumps instantly to the new value.
issues.fix_guidance: In drawPlayerPanel(), use anim.playerDisplayExp instead of pokemon.experience to calculate expPct. In BattleRenderer, lerp anim.playerDisplayExp toward battle.playerPokemon.experience over 600ms (analogous to the existing HP lerp with duration=600). This creates the animated EXP fill on level-up.

issues.description: Accuracy check is not implemented. The task explicitly requires: 'Accuracy check: floor(moveAccuracy * accStage * evaStage) vs random 0–255'. Currently all moves always hit, which is incorrect Gen 1 behavior and a stated requirement.
issues.fix_guidance: In calculateDamage() (or in BattleSystemCore.executeAttack()), before computing damage, perform: const accMultiplier = getStageMultiplier(attackerStatStages['accuracy'] ?? 0); const evaMultiplier = getStageMultiplier(defenderStatStages['evasion'] ?? 0); const adjustedAcc = Math.floor(move.accuracy * accMultiplier / evaMultiplier); const hitRoll = Math.floor(Math.random() * 256); if (hitRoll >= adjustedAcc) return MISS event. Return a { hit: false } signal or emit a MISS BattleEvent.

issues.description: Battle phase state (battle.battlePhase) is mutated inside render() via renderIntro(). Setting battle.battlePhase = 'SELECT_ACTION' in a render method is a side-effect anti-pattern that can cause subtle race conditions between update() and render() ticks.
issues.fix_guidance: Move phase transition logic to BattleSystem.update(). Track intro completion as a time-based condition in update() (e.g., if introStartTime + 400 <= now, set phase = SELECT_ACTION). The renderer should only read state, never write it.

issues.description: Line 102: 'const aiMove = getMoveForSlot(state.wildPokemon, aiAction.moveIndex)' is declared but never used in the ITEM handling block. This is dead code.
issues.fix_guidance: Remove the unused aiMove variable declaration on line 102 in the ITEM branch of processTurn().

issues.description: playerPokemon is always created as Bulbasaur Lv.5 regardless of what the player's actual party contains. The task says 'first non-fainted party member'. The party is stored in SaveSystem but is not consulted here.
issues.fix_guidance: Pass the player's party (array of PokemonInstance or saved species/level data) through startEncounter(). Select the first entry where currentHp > 0. Fall back to Bulbasaur only if the party is empty or all fainted (which is an edge case that could show a 'blackout' screen).

summary: The pure-logic layer (BattleSystem, DamageCalc, StatusEffects, CatchSystem, ExpSystem) is well-implemented and all 68 unit tests pass. However, the critical rendering and UX wiring is substantially incomplete. The battle dialog box never updates from events (players see no damage messages, no type-effectiveness text, no level-up announcements). The ITEM action is entirely non-functional in the action menu. All sprite animations required by the spec — attack lunge, defender white flash, enemy entrance slide, faint drop/fade, Pokeball throw arc, and ball wobble — are absent. The EXP bar renders instantaneously rather than animating. These are not polish gaps; they are explicit line-item requirements in the task spec that a user would immediately notice are missing. The verdict is FAIL.
